name: Add CTA-level shared-memory staging for WMMA tiles

summary.name: Add CTA-level shared-memory staging for WMMA tiles
summary.hypothesis: The new WMMA kernel moved math onto Tensor Cores, but each warp still loads its A and B fragments directly from global memory with no cross-warp reuse. The profile now looks memory-led: tensor pipe active is only 4.78%, DRAM/L2 throughput is much higher than SM throughput, and long scoreboard plus MIO throttle stalls dominate. A shared-memory staging path with CTA-level reuse, ideally double-buffered, is the highest-upside next step because it should turn repeated global fragment fetches into reused BF16 tiles that feed multiple warps.
summary.risk: Moderate to high: shared-memory layout, load choreography, and WMMA-compatible tile packing all become more complex, and a bad layout can trade one memory bottleneck for another.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if staged tiles do not materially lower scoreboard or MIO stalls and tensor activity stays near the same level, because then the added shared-memory pipeline is not improving feed rate to the Tensor Cores.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
